description: Enhance task-mgr doctor with --setup flag to audit Claude Code settings (permissions, hooks, skills, CLAUDE.md, project config) and auto-fix issues.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Approach before code. Anticipate edge cases. Consider 2-3 approaches, pick the best
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — Extensible check registry so new checks can be added as functions without changing the orchestrator
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable code, wired in according to the plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Self-critique after code. Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Quality dimensions explicit. Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — read qualityDimensions on every task; know what 'good' looks like before coding
Approach before code — consider 2-3 approaches with tradeoffs, pick the best, then implement
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test; boundaries are where bugs hide
Self-critique after code — review your own implementation for correctness, style, and performance before moving on
Ship solid working code with tests to prove it
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Auto-fix that modifies ~/.claude/settings.json without explicit user confirmation
False positive checks that waste user time on correct configurations
Error messages that don't include a copy-pasteable fix command

globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks
criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing doctor command behavior

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

userStories.title: Initial tests for setup audit types and check infrastructure
userStories.description: Write tests for the new SetupCheck, SetupSeverity, SetupCategory types and the check registry pattern. Tests should verify severity classification, JSON serialization, and the audit orchestrator contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: SetupSeverity ordering (Blocker > Warning > Info > Pass)
Test: SetupCheck serializes to JSON with all fields
Test: SetupAuditResult correctly counts blockers/warnings/passing
Test: empty audit returns all-passing result
Known-bad discriminator: check with fix_command=None still serializes correctly
Tests compile (may use #[ignore] for unimplemented check functions)
userStories.notes: Follow existing output.rs patterns (IssueType, Issue, Fix). The new types go in a new file src/commands/doctor/setup_output.rs or extend output.rs.

correctness: Types must serialize cleanly for --json output
style: Follow existing output.rs patterns: derive Serialize, Debug, Clone

edgeCases: settings.json doesn't exist: check should report Info, not error
guard-destructive.sh has custom modifications: only check for LOOP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE bypass, don't overwrite
Skills source dir doesn't exist in repo: skip skill installation, only warn about missing global skills
Settings.json has deny rules matching CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS: report as Blocker
Worktree missing .claude/settings.local.json: report as Warning with suggestion
Multiple projects with different tool needs: recommend per-project config over global changes
invariants: Audit never modifies any files (read-only operation)
Every check has a severity, message, and category
Blocker count + Warning count + Pass count = total checks

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Report as Warning with parse error message, don't crash
Skip global checks, report as Warning

userStories.title: Initial tests for individual check functions
userStories.description: Write tests for each check function: defaultMode check, deny conflict check, hook bypass check, skills check, project config check, CLAUDE.md check. Use temp directories with controlled settings files.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: defaultMode 'default' reports Blocker; 'auto' reports Pass
Test: deny rule matching CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS tool reports Blocker
Test: hook without LOOP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE reports Warning
Test: missing skill reports Warning with install command
Test: missing .task-mgr/config.json reports Warning
Test: missing CLAUDE.md reports Info
Test: all checks pass on correctly configured project
Tests compile with temp file fixtures
userStories.notes: Create test fixtures with controlled settings.json files in temp directories. Use std::env::set_var cautiously for HOME override in tests (or pass paths explicitly).

correctness: Each check must be tested in both pass and fail states

edgeCases: settings.json doesn't exist: should not crash, report Info
settings.json is empty object {}: should report defaultMode missing as Pass (no defaultMode = OK)
hook file doesn't exist: should report Info about missing hook
hook file exists but isn't readable: should report Warning with permission error
invariants: No check function should panic on missing/malformed input
Every check returns a SetupCheck with all required fields populated

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Graceful degradation — report what we can check, skip what we can't

userStories.title: Setup audit types and result structs
userStories.description: Create the new types for the setup audit: SetupSeverity, SetupCategory, SetupCheck, SetupAuditResult, SetupFix. Add to a new file src/commands/doctor/setup_output.rs. Follow existing output.rs patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: SetupSeverity enum: Blocker, Warning, Info, Pass (with Serialize, Debug, Clone)
SetupCategory enum: Permissions, Hooks, Skills, ProjectConfig, Documentation
SetupCheck struct: name, category, severity, message, fix_command (Option<String>), auto_fixable (bool)
SetupAuditResult struct: checks Vec, blockers/warnings/passing counts
SetupFix struct: name, action, success (bool)
format_setup_text() function for human-readable output
All types implement Serialize for --json output
TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
userStories.notes: Keep output types separate from check logic. The format function should use colors: red for Blocker, yellow for Warning, cyan for Info, green for Pass.

correctness: Serialization must produce clean JSON for tooling integration
style: Follow output.rs patterns exactly: derive macros, Display impl, format functions

userStories.title: Individual check functions for settings audit
userStories.description: Implement check functions in a new file src/commands/doctor/setup_checks.rs. Each function takes paths/config and returns a SetupCheck. Checks: defaultMode, deny conflicts, ask conflicts, hook bypass, skills installed, project config, CLAUDE.md.
acceptanceCriteria: check_default_mode(settings_path) -> SetupCheck: reads settings.json, checks defaultMode field
check_deny_conflicts(settings_path) -> Vec<SetupCheck>: checks deny rules against CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS
check_hook_bypass(hook_path) -> SetupCheck: greps hook file for LOOP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE
check_skills_installed(global_dir, expected) -> Vec<SetupCheck>: checks each skill file exists
check_project_config(db_dir) -> SetupCheck: checks .task-mgr/config.json exists
check_claude_md(project_dir) -> SetupCheck: checks CLAUDE.md exists
Each check returns appropriate severity and copy-pasteable fix_command
TEST-INIT-002 tests pass
userStories.notes: Import CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS from loop_engine::config (make it pub if not already). Parse settings.json with serde_json::Value (don't need full typed struct — settings format may change). For hook check, read file as string and grep for the bypass pattern. Reference EXPECTED_GLOBAL_SKILLS from engine.rs.

correctness: Must never crash on missing/malformed files — graceful degradation
Must correctly identify defaultMode: 'default' as problematic
performance: Each check should return in <10ms (local file reads only)
style: Pure functions: path in, SetupCheck out. No side effects in check functions.

userStories.title: Setup audit orchestrator and --setup CLI flag
userStories.description: Wire the check functions into an audit_setup() orchestrator function. Add --setup flag to the Doctor CLI command. When --setup is passed, run setup checks. When neither --setup nor DB flags, run both.
acceptanceCriteria: audit_setup(project_dir) -> SetupAuditResult: runs all checks, collects results
CLI: --setup flag added to Doctor command in commands.rs
main.rs: dispatches to setup audit when --setup is passed
Output: human-readable summary with severity-colored checks
Output: --json flag produces machine-readable JSON (reuse existing --format pattern if available)
When no flags: runs both DB health checks and setup checks
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: The orchestrator determines paths: ~/.claude/settings.json, ~/.claude/hooks/guard-destructive.sh, ~/.claude/commands/, .task-mgr/config.json, CLAUDE.md. Use dirs::home_dir() or std::env::var('HOME').

correctness: Must determine correct paths for all platforms (Linux primarily)
style: Follow existing doctor() function pattern: collect issues, optionally fix, return result

userStories.title: Auto-fix engine for repairable issues
userStories.description: Implement auto-fix functions for issues that can be repaired automatically: install missing skills, generate .task-mgr/config.json, patch guard-destructive.sh hook, generate template CLAUDE.md. Never auto-modify ~/.claude/settings.json.
acceptanceCriteria: fix_install_skills(source_dir, global_dir, missing_skills) -> Vec<SetupFix>: copies .md files
fix_generate_project_config(db_dir, detected_tools) -> SetupFix: writes config.json
fix_patch_hook(hook_path) -> SetupFix: adds LOOP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE bypass, creates .bak backup
fix_generate_claude_md(project_dir, db_path) -> SetupFix: generates template CLAUDE.md
Auto-fix is idempotent: running twice produces same result
Settings.json suggestions printed to stderr but NOT auto-applied
Hook patch preserves all existing checks, only adds bypass at top
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: For hook patching: read the file, check if bypass already exists, if not insert after the #!/bin/bash line and before the first command. Create .bak backup before modifying. For project config auto-detection: check for .proto files -> add protoc, Dockerfile -> add docker, pyproject.toml -> add python3/uv, etc.

correctness: Hook patch must preserve existing checks
style: Each fix function is pure: takes inputs, returns result. Side effects (file writes) happen inside the function but are clearly documented.

userStories.title: Loop start pre-check for new task lists
userStories.description: Add a lightweight setup pre-check at loop startup that runs only when starting a new task list (not resuming). Checks blocker-level issues only and prints a warning banner.
acceptanceCriteria: pre_check_loop_setup(dir) -> Vec<SetupCheck>: runs blocker checks only (defaultMode, deny conflicts)
Called in engine.rs run_loop() during startup sequence, after check_global_skills
Only runs when is_new_task_list is true (not on resume)
Prints yellow warning banner if blockers found
Non-blocking: loop continues regardless of check results
Adds <100ms to startup time
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: Determine 'new vs resume' by checking if iteration_counter > 0 in global_state or if there's an existing run for this PRD. The check should be fast — only read settings.json and check defaultMode + deny rules. Skip skills/config/CLAUDE.md checks (those are for the full --setup audit).

correctness: Must correctly distinguish new task list from resume
Non-blocking — must not prevent loop from starting
style: Follow existing check_global_skills pattern in engine.rs for warning output

userStories.title: Review implementation for quality, security, and integration
userStories.description: Review all implementation tasks for code quality, error handling, security (especially file operations in auto-fix), and verify integration wiring.
acceptanceCriteria: No unwrap() in production code paths
All errors properly propagated with context
Security: auto-fix doesn't follow symlinks or write outside expected paths
Security: hook patch preserves existing checks
Security: settings.json contents never logged
All new code is exported and wired into the doctor command
Any issues found have corresponding CODE-FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.notes: Pay special attention to file write operations in setup_fixes.rs. Verify symlink safety. Check that the hook backup (.bak) is created before modification.

userStories.title: Review implementation for refactoring opportunities
userStories.description: Analyze implementation code for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability.
acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication (DRY principle)
Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones)
Check functions follow consistent pattern (extensible registry)
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-1-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.notes: Focus on check function consistency. All checks should follow the same input/output pattern for easy extensibility.

userStories.description: All check functions, auto-fix, CLI wiring, and loop pre-check implemented. Verify everything works together.

Test: hook patch preserves existing custom checks

80%+ coverage for setup_checks.rs and setup_fixes.rs
